channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Graffiti sprayed on shop shutter next to 226 Rafi Barber shop Brixton road SW9., 10th May
item.description:
A graffiti tag has been sprayed on a shop shutter next to Rafi Barber shop 226 Brixton road SW9. The graffiti has been there for approximately three weeks. Unfortunately there has been a surge again along Brixton main road. Please can the graffiti be removed it be very much appreciated.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 238, Brixton Road, Oval, City of London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rufus House, ST Saviours Estate, Abbey Street, 10th May
item.description:
We found the graffiti on staircase window. Please paint it.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 60, Maltby Street, Old Kent Road, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 25 harvist road, 10th May
item.description:
Overgrown plant occupying half of the pedestrian pathway
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Harvist Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Litter, 10th May
item.description:
Brook Vale, Becton place and York Terrace have an excessive amount of litter strewn all over the pavements, and in curbs and roads. Litter pickers are needed
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Brook Vale, Northumberland Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 23 harvist road, 10th May
item.description:
There is a persistent accumulation of household waste overflowing in the front area of the property. Although most of the waste is within the property boundary, it is heavily overfilled, creating concerns regarding vermin, odour, hygiene, and waste dispersal into the public area. The issue appears ongoing rather than a one-off missed collection.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Harvist Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned Caravan in a dangerous junction location, 10th May
item.description:
Abandoned Caravan in a dangerous location at the junction. Extremely dangerous due to already narrow roads especially for passing buses and bigger vehicles. Drivers coming up on the same side as the parked caravan do not have a clear view of on-coming vehicles to safely overtake due to its huge size. This is an accident location waiting to happen. There is a children nursery at the same junction and creating hazards for parents and children. The caravan has been in the same spot for weeks! Urgent action must be taken to remove the caravan. In fact, the row of vehicles parked along this high street road is creating hazard on a daily basis due to already narrow roads. Thank you

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 191, High Street, Poverest, Orpington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Litter and debris, 10th May
item.description:
This stretch of White Horse Hill between Telegraph Path & Oakdene Ave is litter strewn on both sides and also on and adjacent to the elevated footpath. Please could the area be litter picked and kept tidy especially after weekly bin collections? Thank you
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Red Hill, Chislehurst, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Outside no. 28, 10th May
item.description:
4 uneven paving stones
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 34, Glenilla Road, Belsize Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Carlton Vale, corner of Neville Close, eastbound cycle lane, 10th May
item.description:
Low tree branches (circa 1.5 m) impeding the cycle lane, posing a risk to cyclists.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Carlton Vale, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped domestic bags, 10th May
item.description:
Dumped bags around the lamp post outside 17A Southwold Road, E5. Also dumped bags near bus stop located near Barberhood Barber Shop on Southwold Road, E5
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Dennington Close, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped wooden planks and flytipping, 10th May
item.description:
Dumped wooden planks outside Sancho House, Southwold Rd (in front of the large metal dustbin). Also, rubbish outside the metal dustbin in front of Cann House, Southwold Rd
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Southwold Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: A van dumped a lot of garden waste/branches, 10th May
item.description:
I saw a van emptying branches/garden waste onto the side of the road here

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Dando Crescent, Kidbrooke
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping, 10th May
item.description:
Dumped washing machine on street. Dumped mattresses and wood in garden of 110 George lane.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): George Lane, Hither Green, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pavements and streets filthy 365 days a year., 10th May
item.description:
The entirety of Queen’s Park south of the bridge at the station is HORRIBLE, it hasn’t been cleaned in days. There’s potholes everywhere. I’ve attached a picture taken in front of M&S to show the constant state, it’s just an example. Please check the edges with plants too, they are filthy. This is why people swing to opposite political parties. You. Are. Not. Doing. Your. Jobs. Why exactly are we paying council tax? This is were you need to start. Make tax payers’ life better. Not just social causes. Just be better. This is filthy. People shouldn’t live this way.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Albert Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping, 10th May
item.description:
Once again, fly tipping outside our flat

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Foulden Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly tipping every single day., 10th May
item.description:
As before, and before that. i could send you a similar picture almost every day. please, please, please, tackle the root causes. many thanks

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Stoke Newington Church Street, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned vehicle, 10th May
item.description:
Been here for a long time, stolen maybe

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Kidd Place, Charlton
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Southbound cycle lane on Royal College Street, from opposite number 89 down past the Veterinary College, 10th May
item.description:
Three drain grates are not covering their drains at all, leaving very deep holes in the road uncovered. This is extremely dangerous; if my wheel went into any of these, I would go over the handlebars immediately. The grate opposite number 89 is somewhat covering its drain, but leaving a hole that's still dangerous, while a grate nearer the junction with Crowndale Road is covering its hole but sticking up by a couple of inches, which would be dangerous if run over.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Royal College Street, St Pancras, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dead Animal, 10th May
item.description:
In the middle of the road
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Kashmir Road, Charlton
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Sinking road, 10th May
item.description:
The Stretch of road is now sinking as what happens before a sink hole in many places. If an emergency service vehicle was to go down at any speed it would be a danger. This section of road should be addressed as a matter of urgency due to potential to harm people and vehicles.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Brixham Road, West Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
